title: 10 best herbal creams for endometriosis pain

remedy_properties: Curcumin: Curcumin, a bioactive compound in turmeric, has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate endometriosis-related pain.

Corticosteroid-like compounds: Turmeric contains corticosteroid-like compounds that can help reduce swelling and inflammation in the pelvic area, providing relief from endometriosis symptoms such as cramping and bloating.

COX-1/COX-2 inhibition: Turmeric's curcuminoids have been shown to inhibit COX-1 and COX-2 enzymes, which are responsible for producing prostaglandins that cause pain and inflammation in endometriosis, thereby providing natural pain relief.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with endometriosis pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric cream has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with endometriosis. By alleviating inflammation and promoting healing, turmeric creams can provide relief from chronic pelvic pain, menstrual cramps, and heavy bleeding commonly experienced by women with endometriosis. The natural anti-inflammatory effects of turmeric creams offer a promising alternative for managing endometriosis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: Curcumin, a polyphenolic compound, helps alleviate endometriosis pain by reducing inflammation and modulating the body's immune response, which can contribute to the development and progression of endometriosis.

Beta-caryophyllene: Beta-caryophyllene, a sesquiterpene, exhibits anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate the chronic pain associated with endometriosis by suppressing the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, a curcuminoid, has been found to inhibit the growth of endometrial cells, reduce inflammation, and alleviate pain, making it a potential compound to help manage endometriosis symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the most commonly used part due to their high concentration of curcumin, a compound known for its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa also contain curcumin, making them a popular choice for creams aimed at reducing endometriosis pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 kg of fresh curcuma longa rhizomes and wash them thoroughly with cold water.
Dry the washed curcuma longa rhizomes in a hot air oven at 50°c for 24 hours.
Grind 100g of dried curcuma longa rhizomes into a fine powder using a spice grinder.
Mix 50g of curcuma longa powder with 100g of coconut oil and 50g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes and then let it cool and solidify.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pelvic pain associated with endometriosis.
Pain-relieving properties: The analgesic and antispasmodic effects of herbal ginger creams can help to numb the pain caused by endometriosis, providing relief from cramps and discomfort.

Digestive benefits: Ginger's ability to soothe digestive issues, such as bloating and nausea, can help alleviate symptoms of endometriosis, including irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) and other gastrointestinal complaints.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with endometriosis pain because of its anti-inflammatory properties. It contains compounds like gingerols and shogaols that reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the affected areas. The cream's topical application allows for direct absorption, providing quick relief from endometriosis-related discomfort. Additionally, ginger's natural analgesic and antispasmodic properties help to relax muscles and reduce cramping, making it a promising natural remedy for managing endometriosis symptoms. This can provide relief for many women.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These sesquiterpene compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce pain and inflammation associated with endometriosis.
Shogaols: Derivatives of gingerols, shogaols are also known for their anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, which can help alleviate endometriosis-related pain and discomfort.

Caryophyllene oxide: A sesquiterpene oxide, caryophyllene oxide has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with endometriosis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of ginger due to their high concentration of active compounds, particularly gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate endometriosis pain.
Roots: Roots of ginger contain a high amount of gingerol, which is converted to shogaol during processing, making them a popular choice for pain-relieving creams.
Leaves: Ginger leaves are also used in traditional medicine due to their high content of volatile oils, which have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce endometriosis pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 zingiber officinale roots with the highest essential oil content in their peak season.
Clean the harvested roots with cold water to remove any dirt or debris thoroughly.
Grate 200g of cleaned zingiber officinale roots to release their active compounds quickly.
Mix 20g of grated zingiber officinale with 100g of coconut oil in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Add 10g of beeswax to the mixture and heat it for another 20 minutes to achieve a smooth consistency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbs like chamomile and ginger have anti-inflammatory compounds that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with endometriosis.
Analgesic properties: Herbs like lavender and chamomile contain analgesic compounds that may help numb or block pain, providing relief from endometriosis-related cramps and discomfort.
Relaxing and calming effects: Herbs like chamomile and lavender have a calming effect on the body, which may help reduce stress and anxiety related to endometriosis symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Capsicum annuum, also known as bell pepper, creams helps with endometriosis pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The capsaicin in these creams interacts with pain receptors, blocking the transmission of pain signals to the brain. This leads to a reduction in prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that cause inflammation and pain in endometriosis. The result is a decrease in menstrual cramps and pelvic pain associated with endometriosis, providing relief to individuals suffering from this condition. This natural remedy offers a promising alternative for managing endometriosis pain.
remedy_constituents: Capsaicin: Capsaicin helps reduce endometriosis pain by blocking the release of pain-causing chemicals, such as substance P, from nerve endings and increasing the production of pain-relieving chemicals.

Quercetin: Quercetin, a flavonoid phenolic compound, has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and pain associated with endometriosis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Cinnamaldehyde: Cinnamaldehyde, an aromatic aldehyde found in Capsicum annuum, has analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that may help alleviate endometriosis pain by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals and reducing inflammation.
remedy_parts: Fruits: Capsaicin, found in the fruits of Capsicum annuum, is used to create creams that relieve pain by blocking the production of a chemical called substance P, which transmits pain signals to the brain.
Leaves: The leaves of Capsicum annuum contain capsaicin and other compounds, which are extracted to create creams that help reduce inflammation and alleviate endometriosis pain.
Seeds: Capsaicin is also found in the seeds of Capsicum annuum, and it's often used to create creams that help reduce pain by blocking the production of substance P and reducing inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-15 fresh capsicum annuum fruits when they are fully ripe and have reached their peak color.
Clean the capsicum annuum fruits under cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Dry the cleaned capsicum annuum fruits in a single layer at 35°c for 24-48 hours to reduce moisture content.
Extract the oil from the dried capsicum annuum fruits using a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin at a ratio of 1:10.
Mix 10% of the extracted capsicum annuum oil with 90% of a base cream such as shea butter and coconut oil to create the final product.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Arnica contains sesquiterpenes, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with endometriosis.

Pain-relieving flavonoids: The flavonoids present in arnica, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have analgesic and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in reducing chronic pelvic pain and cramping associated with endometriosis.
Antioxidant activity: Arnica's antioxidant properties help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to the development of endometriosis and worsen symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Arnica montana, also known as mountain arnica, creams helps with endometriosis pain because of its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The cream contains compounds like sesquiterpene lactones, which reduce inflammation and swelling in the affected areas. This helps to alleviate the chronic pain associated with endometriosis. Additionally, the cream's topical application allows for direct penetration of the active ingredients, targeting the pain at its source and providing fast-acting relief. Regular use may also help to reduce overall discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Alkamides: These sesquiterpene lactones exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help alleviate endometriosis pain by reducing inflammation and modulating pain perception.

Flavonoids: These phenolic compounds, such as kaempferol and quercetin, have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help mitigate endometriosis-related inflammation and oxidative stress, contributing to pain relief.

Tanshinones: Although less common in Arnica montana, tanshinones have been reported to be present in some herbal extracts. Their anti-inflammatory and anti-angiogenic properties might help reduce inflammation and alleviate endometriosis pain.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are rich in bioactive compounds, including sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.
Leaves: They contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to alleviate endometriosis pain.

Buds: The buds contain a high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, which have been shown to have potent anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them a valuable ingredient in creams for endometriosis pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh arnica montana flowers in early morning after the dew has evaporated for maximum potency within 2 hours.
Dry the arnica montana flowers in a single layer on paper towels for 2 weeks at room temperature.
Combine 200g of dried arnica montana flowers with 500ml of olive oil in a clean glass jar for infusion.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day for optimal extraction.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and whip 250g of beeswax with 50g of cocoa butter for thickening and emollience.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort creams contain antioxidants like hyperforin, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, alleviating endometriosis-related pain.

Analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties: The active compounds in St. John's Wort, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects, providing relief from chronic pelvic pain associated with endometriosis.

Serotonin regulation: St. John's Wort creams may help regulate serotonin levels, a neurotransmitter involved in pain modulation, which can contribute to the management of endometriosis-related pain and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with endometriosis pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The active compounds in Hypericum, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax smooth muscle, which can help alleviate cramping and discomfort associated with endometriosis. Additionally, the creams may help to reduce prostaglandins, hormone-like substances that can contribute to pain and inflammation in endometriosis sufferers. This natural remedy may provide relief from endometriosis symptoms, promoting a better quality of life.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: A phenolic derivative, hyperforin has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with endometriosis.

Naringenin: A flavonoid, naringenin has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, potentially providing relief from endometriosis pain.

Quercetin: A flavonoid and a potent antioxidant, quercetin may help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and scavenging free radicals, which can contribute to endometriosis pain.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh hypericum perforatum flowers in the early morning to ensure optimal potency.
Dry the fresh flowers in a warm and dry place for 7-10 days to maintain their medicinal properties.
Combine 50g of dried hypericum perforatum flowers with 50g of beeswax and 10g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 30 minutes while stirring occasionally until the beeswax has melted.
Remove the mixture from the heat and allow it to cool and thicken for 2 hours before transferring it to a jar.

remedy_properties: Vasodilatory properties: Guelder rose's ability to dilate blood vessels can help reduce inflammation and relax spasms in the uterus, alleviating cramps and pelvic discomfort associated with endometriosis.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Guelder rose contains flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce the chronic inflammation that contributes to endometriosis pain and discomfort.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Viburnum opulus, also known as guelder rose, creams helps with endometriosis pain because it contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. Additionally, Viburnum opulus has been shown to have antispasmodic properties, which can help to ease cramping and discomfort. Flavonoids (e.g., quercetin): Quercetin and other flavonoids present in Viburnum opulus may help reduce inflammation and pain by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, as well as modulating the body's response to pain.

Phenolic acids (e.g., ferulic acid): Phenolic acids in the plant may help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain by scavenging free radicals, modulating the immune response, and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and mediators.
remedy_parts: Buds: They contain bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and terpenoids, which may help reduce inflammation and pain associated with endometriosis.
Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and other polyphenols, they may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain through their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Fruits: The fruits of Viburnum opulus contain anthocyanins, which may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250 grams of dried viburnum opulus flowers at the peak of blooming to ensure maximum potency and freshness.
Combine the dried flowers with 500 milliliters of carrier oil in a heat-proof glass container such as a jar.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 40 degrees celsius for 2 hours to facilitate oil extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to separate the oil from the solids.
Add 50 grams of beeswax and 20 grams of cocoa butter to the extracted oil and heat the mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dong quai cream's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the pelvis and ovaries, which can contribute to endometriosis pain.

Butylphthalide: A sesquiterpene that has been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects, helping to reduce pain and inflammation in endometriosis patients.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in ferulic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce endometriosis-related pain.
Stems: Contain a compound called ligustilide, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects, making it useful for endometriosis creams.
Leaves: Rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate endometriosis symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried angelica sinensis roots and soak them in 100ml of cold water for 30 minutes.
Steep the angelica sinensis mixture for 2 hours in a double boiler at 40-50°c to release its essence.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth to remove the angelica sinensis residue and discard the solids.
Mix the strained liquid with 100g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Whip the mixture with a hand mixer until it thickens and forms a smooth cream consistency.

remedy_properties: Sedative and anxiolytic properties: Valerian's calming effects can help reduce anxiety, stress, and promote relaxation, which can alleviate menstrual cramps and discomfort associated with endometriosis.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian, creams helps with endometriosis pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. remedy_constituents: Valerenic acid: Valerenic acid, a sesquiterpene, may help reduce endometriosis pain by modulating the GABA neurotransmitter system, leading to a calming effect on the nervous system and reduced inflammation.

Valeranone: Valeranone, a sesquiterpene, has been found to have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate endometriosis pain by reducing prostaglandin production and modulating the pain response.

Leaves: The leaves are used because they contain valerenol, a sesquiterpene that has been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help manage endometriosis pain.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried valeriana officinalis roots in late summer or early fall when the plant is mature.
Infuse 10 grams of the dried roots in 100ml of jojoba oil in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 30 minutes of steeping.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of shea butter in a double boiler to melt slowly over low heat.
Combine the infused oil with the melted beeswax and shea butter mixture and stir until well combined.

remedy_properties: Antispasmodic property: Lavender's antispasmodic effects can help reduce menstrual cramps and spasms, providing relief from abdominal pain associated with endometriosis.
Anti-inflammatory property: Lavender's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the pelvic region, which is a common symptom of endometriosis and contributes to chronic pain.
Sedative property: Lavender's sedative effects can promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, which are often comorbid with endometriosis pain, helping to alleviate overall discomfort and distress.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia, also known as English lavender, creams helps with endometriosis pain because of its anti-inflammatory and relaxing properties. The linalool and linalyl acetate present in lavender oil have been shown to reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms associated with endometriosis. Additionally, the calming scent of lavender oil can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common comorbidities with endometriosis. remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with endometriosis.
Linalyl acetate: This terpene has a calming effect on the body, reducing stress and anxiety that can exacerbate endometriosis pain, and also has anti-inflammatory properties.

remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties.

Stems: While less commonly mentioned, some sources suggest that the stems of Lavandula angustifolia may also contain beneficial compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which could contribute to their anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak potency for optimal essential oil extraction.
Combine 1/2 cup of dried flowers with 2 cups of carrier oil in a clean glass jar for infusion.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place with occasional gentle shaking and stirring.
Strain the oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain the infused oil.
Mix 1/4 cup of the infused oil with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of shea butter in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin, reduce inflammation and swelling in the pelvic area, alleviating pain and discomfort associated with endometriosis.
Muscle relaxant: The calcium channel-blocking properties of chamomile help to relax uterine muscles, reducing cramping and spasms that can exacerbate endometriosis symptoms.
Anxiolytic: Chamomile's calming effects on the nervous system reduce anxiety and stress, which can be significant contributors to endometriosis pain and discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Matricaria chamomilla, also known as chamomile, creams helps with endometriosis pain because it contains anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that soothe and calm the uterine muscles, reducing cramps and discomfort. remedy_constituents: Apigenin: This flavonoid acts as a potent anti-inflammatory agent, reducing pain and inflammation associated with endometriosis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Luteolin: This flavonoid has been shown to possess anti-angiogenic properties, which may help reduce the growth of endometrial lesions and alleviate pain by preventing the formation of new blood vessels that supply them.
α-bisabolol: This sesquiterpene has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which may contribute to its pain-relieving effects by reducing inflammation and modulating pain perception in the body.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties help to reduce pain and inflammation associated with endometriosis.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of dried flowers in the morning after the dew has evaporated for best results.
Infuse the flowers in 2 cups of boiling water for 5 minutes to release their medicinal properties.
Strain the liquid and combine it with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil.
Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring constantly, for 10 minutes or until the beeswax melts.
Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover creams helps with endometriosis pain because they contain isoflavones, plant-based compounds that mimic the effects of estrogen in the body. These isoflavones help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with endometriosis, such as pelvic pain and heavy bleeding. They also have a natural anti-inflammatory effect, which can help to relax uterine muscles and reduce cramping. By reducing inflammation and alleviating discomfort, red clover creams provide relief from the chronic pain and discomfort that often accompanies endometriosis.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover cream contains isoflavones, which have anti-inflammatory and estrogen-regulating effects, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of endometriosis such as pelvic pain and cramping.

Saponins: Saponins in red clover cream have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, reducing the growth of endometrial tissue outside the uterus and alleviating related pain and discomfort.

Phytoestrogens: Phytoestrogens in red clover cream mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, helping to regulate hormonal imbalances that can contribute to endometriosis symptoms such as heavy bleeding and cramping.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in isoflavones, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with endometriosis.

Flowers: Contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds like formononetin and biochanin A, which have anti-inflammatory and estrogen-regulating properties beneficial for endometriosis pain relief.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried red clover flowers at a ratio of 2 parts to 1 part carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil and store for up to 6 months.
Infuse the dried red clover flowers in the carrier oil by soaking them in a cool dark place for 2 weeks with gentle agitation daily.
Strain the infused oil mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container discarding the solids after 24 hours.
Mix the infused oil with beeswax and vitamin e oil in a ratio of 7 parts oil to 1 part wax and melt at low heat for 10 minutes.
Pour the melted mixture into small tin containers and let cool until solidified taking approximately 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blue cohosh creams helps with endometriosis pain because they contain a natural compound that relaxes the uterine muscles, reducing cramping and spasms. Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in blue cohosh creams reduce inflammation and swelling in the pelvic area, which can contribute to pain and discomfort caused by endometriosis.

Rhyzomes: Contain berberine, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps reduce swelling and pain caused by endometriosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh caulophyllum thalictroides roots and dry them for at least 24 hours before use.
Combine 100 grams of dried roots with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil.
Steep the root mixture in a warm location for 8-10 days, shaking the container daily, to extract the active compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids, then add 20 grams of beeswax and heat until melted.
Pour the cooled mixture into sterilized containers and store them in an airtight jar for up to six months or until used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild yam creams helps with endometriosis pain because they contain saponins, a natural compound that mimics the hormone progesterone. This can help to reduce inflammation and relax uterine muscle contractions, thereby alleviating menstrual cramps, bloating, and other symptoms associated with endometriosis. The cream also increases prostaglandin levels, which can help to soften and thin the uterus lining, making menstruation more manageable and reducing the severity of pain. Regular use of herbal wild yam creams may provide long-term relief from endometriosis-related discomfort.
remedy_properties: Estrogenic compounds: Wild yam contains estrogen-like compounds called saponins, which may help regulate hormonal imbalances associated with endometriosis and reduce symptoms such as cramps and heavy bleeding.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The creams may contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce inflammation and pain associated with endometriosis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals in the body.

Pain-relieving and analgesic properties: Wild yam has been traditionally used as a natural pain reliever, which can help alleviate menstrual cramps, lower back pain, and other symptoms associated with endometriosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in diosgenin, a phytochemical that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate cramps associated with endometriosis.
Rhyzomes: Contain a high concentration of saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help to soothe menstrual cramps and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dioscorea villosa roots and organic coconut oil, shea butter, beeswax, and vitamin e oil.
Dry the dioscorea villosa roots for 24 hours in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator on low heat.
Grind 30 grams of dried dioscorea villosa roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Melt 100 grams of coconut oil, 50 grams of shea butter, and 25 grams of beeswax in a double boiler over low heat for 10 minutes, then stir in the ground dioscorea villosa root powder.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool slightly before whipping with a hand mixer or whisk for 5 minutes until thickened into a smooth cream consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Safflower creams helps with endometriosis pain because it contains a unique compound called alpha-linolenic acid (ALA), which has potent anti-inflammatory properties. ALA works to reduce inflammation and alleviate cramps, heavy bleeding, and other symptoms associated with endometriosis. Additionally, safflower creams may help to relax uterine muscle contractions, further decreasing discomfort and pain. By targeting the root causes of endometriosis-related pain, safflower creams offer a natural and effective way to manage symptoms and improve overall quality of life for those affected by this chronic condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Safflower creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like omega-6 fatty acids, which can help reduce inflammation in the body, alleviating pelvic pain and cramping associated with endometriosis.
Pain-relieving properties: Herbal safflower creams may contain analgesic and anesthetic properties that can help relieve menstrual cramps, back pain, and other discomforts caused by endometriosis.

Antioxidant and free-radical scavenging abilities: Safflower's antioxidants can help neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress in the body, which may contribute to reduced inflammation and alleviated symptoms of endometriosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, the roots are effective in reducing inflammation and pain associated with endometriosis.
Seeds: The seeds contain essential fatty acids and antioxidants that help alleviate symptoms of endometriosis by improving hormonal balance.
remedy_recipe: Harvest carthamus tinctorius flowers in bulk and air-dry them for at least two weeks to reduce moisture content.
Combine dried carthamus tinctorius flowers with coconut oil in a 1:3 ratio by weight and steep the mixture overnight for eight hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the solids and reserve the liquid extract.
Mix the carthamus tinctorius extract with beeswax and shea butter in equal proportions by weight, then heat the blend gently until melted and combined.
Pour the creamy mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature, taking about four hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with endometriosis pain because they provide a natural and effective way to alleviate symptoms associated with the condition. The menthol in peppermint has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation and relax muscles, which can ease menstrual cramps, bloating, and digestive discomfort often experienced by women with endometriosis. Additionally, peppermint's calming effects can promote relaxation and reduce stress, further contributing to a decrease in overall pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as menthol and menthone, help to reduce inflammation in the pelvic area, which can contribute to endometriosis pain.

Analgesic and anesthetic properties: The analgesic (pain-relieving) and anesthetic properties of peppermint help to numb the pain associated with endometriosis, providing relief from cramping, bloating, and discomfort.
Spasmolytic properties: Peppermint's ability to relax smooth muscle tissue can help to ease spasms and cramps in the uterus, pelvis, and bowels, which are common symptoms of endometriosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Peppermint roots contain menthol and other active compounds that help to reduce inflammation and relax muscles, providing relief from endometriosis-related pelvic pain.

Rhyzomes: The rhizome of the peppermint plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective in reducing symptoms of endometriosis such as cramps, bloating, and mood swings.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality peppermint leaves in the morning after dew has dried.
Steam distill 200g of fresh peppermint leaves for 30 minutes using 500ml water.
Combine 20g of steamed peppermint essential oil with 100g of organic coconut oil and 50g of beeswax.
Mix well for 10 minutes until the ingredients are fully incorporated and melted at room temperature.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass jar and let it solidify for 30 minutes before using.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew creams helps with endometriosis pain because of its ability to reduce inflammation and relax muscles. The natural compounds in feverfew, such as parthenolide, have been shown to inhibit the production of prostaglandins, which are hormone-like substances that cause muscle contractions and contribute to pain and cramping associated with endometriosis. Additionally, feverfew's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the pelvic area, providing relief from menstrual cramps, bloating, and heavy bleeding common in endometriosis sufferers.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Feverfew contains parthenolide, which has potent anti-inflammatory effects, helping to reduce inflammation and swelling in the pelvic area caused by endometriosis.

Pain-relieving flavonoids: Feverfew also contains flavonoids like apigenin and luteolin, which have analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties, providing relief from cramps, discomfort, and pain associated with endometriosis.
Hormone-regulating sesquiterpenes: Feverfew's sesquiterpene compounds may help regulate hormone levels, including estrogen, which can contribute to the development and progression of endometriosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds that help alleviate inflammation and reduce prostaglandins, contributing to relief from endometriosis pain.
Leaves: Containing parthenolide, a sesquiterpene lactone with anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making it effective for treating endometriosis-related discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium flowers and 20 grams of coconut oil for preparation.
Heat the coconut oil in a double boiler at 60 degrees celsius for 30 minutes to create a base.
Add 5 grams of beeswax to the heated coconut oil and stir until it reaches a temperature of 80 degrees celsius.
Infuse the mixture with the dried tanacetum parthenium flowers by letting it steep at room temperature for 2 hours.
Filter the mixture using cheesecloth and let it cool before transferring it into sterilized containers for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with endometriosis pain because of its unique blend of herbal extracts, rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds. The cream's key ingredient, Maidenhair fern, has been traditionally used to ease menstrual discomfort and reduce inflammation. Additionally, other herbs like Willow bark and Ginger have natural analgesic and anti-spasmodic properties, which help to alleviate cramping and pelvic pain associated with endometriosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate cramping associated with endometriosis.
Barks: Contain flavonoids that have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in relieving pelvic pain caused by endometriosis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh ginkgo biloba leaves in the autumn season for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested leaves by spreading them thinly on paper towels for 7-10 days.
Grind the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix 2 tablespoons of coconut oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 2 teaspoons of powdered ginkgo biloba in a double boiler.
Remove from heat and let cool for 30 minutes before whipping the mixture with an electric mixer until smooth and creamy.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic properties: Maidenhair Tree (Ginkgo biloba) has adaptogenic properties that help the body adapt to stress and inflammation, which can alleviate endometriosis-related pain by reducing oxidative stress and promoting a balanced hormonal environment.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Maidenhair Tree contains flavonoids and terpenes that exhibit anti-inflammatory activity, which may help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with endometriosis by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidant properties of Maidenhair Tree may help neutralize free radicals and oxidative stress, which can contribute to pain and inflammation in endometriosis patients by protecting against cellular damage and promoting healthy tissue function.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Parsley creams helps with endometriosis pain because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The cream's active compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been shown to reduce inflammation in the pelvic area, alleviating symptoms like cramping, bloating, and heavy bleeding associated with endometriosis. Additionally, parsley's antioxidants help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate pain and discomfort. By targeting both inflammation and oxidative stress, parsley creams may provide natural relief from endometriosis-related pain, allowing women to manage their condition more effectively.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Parsley contains anti-inflammatory compounds like apigenin, which can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pelvic pain associated with endometriosis.
Antioxidant properties: The high antioxidant content in parsley cream may help protect the body from oxidative stress caused by endometrial tissue growth, reducing inflammation and discomfort.

Estrogen-regulating flavonoids: Parsley contains flavonoids like luteolin, which have been shown to regulate estrogen levels and reduce symptoms of endometriosis, such as pain, heavy bleeding, and mood swings.
remedy_parts: Roots: Parsley roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help to reduce swelling and pain associated with endometriosis.
Seeds: The seeds of parsley contain essential fatty acids that can help to soothe and calm inflammation in the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest petroselinum crispum leaves in the morning after the dew has dried.
Dry the harvested leaves by spreading them thinly on paper towels for 24 hours.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine 20g of powdered petroselinum crispum with 100ml of coconut oil and 10g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 70°c for 30 minutes then strain it through a cheesecloth into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage creams helps with endometriosis pain because they contain compounds that have a natural anti-inflammatory effect, reducing inflammation in the pelvic area. This can help alleviate cramps, bloating, and discomfort associated with the condition. Additionally, sage's antioxidant properties may also help to reduce oxidative stress, which can contribute to chronic pain and discomfort experienced by women with endometriosis. By applying topical cream containing sage, patients may find relief from their symptoms, improving their overall quality of life.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Sage contains compounds like rosmarinic acid and camphor, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and swelling associated with endometriosis, thereby alleviating pelvic pain.

Antispasmodic agents: The volatile oils present in sage, such as camphor and borneol, possess antispasmodic properties that can help relax uterine muscles and alleviate cramping, spasms, and other symptoms of endometriosis-related dysmenorrhea.

Pain-relieving flavonoids: Sage contains flavonoids like luteolin and quercetin, which have been shown to exhibit analgesic and anti-nociceptive properties, helping to reduce pain perception and alleviate discomfort associated with endometriosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the sage plant are rich in bioactive compounds that help alleviate inflammation and reduce pain associated with endometriosis.
Rhyzomes: Sage rhyzomes contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents that soothe cramps and ease menstrual discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry salvia officinalis leaves in shade for two weeks or until completely brittle.
Grind dried salvia officinalis leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle for five minutes.
Mix 20 grams of powdered salvia officinalis with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed olive oil in glass jar for one hour.
Heat mixture at low temperature for two hours while stirring occasionally to infuse oil with herbal properties.
Strain mixture through cheesecloth and discard solids, then fill sterile jars with filtered cream for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Balm-of-gilead creams helps with endometriosis pain because they contain a unique combination of herbal ingredients that work synergistically to reduce inflammation, ease cramps, and relax the uterine muscles. The cream's active compounds, such as boswellia and turmeric, have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate pelvic discomfort and menstrual cramping associated with endometriosis. Additionally, the cream's warming sensation can help increase blood flow and promote relaxation of the uterine muscles, providing relief from chronic pain and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The resinous sap of the poplar tree, used to make Balm of Gilead, contains salicin, a natural anti-inflammatory that helps reduce swelling and pain associated with endometriosis.

Antioxidant properties: The balm's antioxidant compounds help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate inflammation and oxidative stress, which may contribute to endometriosis-related pain and discomfort.

Pain-relieving analgesics: The balm contains a combination of natural analgesics, including salicin and other compounds, that help relieve chronic pelvic pain, cramps, and discomfort associated with endometriosis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, which help reduce pain and swelling associated with endometriosis.
Barks: Containing a high concentration of flavonoids, which have been shown to alleviate cramps and menstrual discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried bark of populus balsamifera, measuring exactly 20 grams per recipe.
Combine the dried bark with 40 milliliters of high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 14 days, shaking the jar daily to enhance extraction.
Strain the liquid through a coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids after pressing.
Mix the filtered liquid with 100 grams of coconut oil and 50 milliliters of beeswax, heating gently until smooth and creamy.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for endometriosis pain are topical treatments that combine natural herbs with moisturizing properties to alleviate pelvic pain associated with the condition. They work by reducing inflammation, relaxing muscles, and improving circulation, providing relief from cramping, bloating, and discomfort. Examples include creams infused with turmeric, ginger, and arnica, which have anti-inflammatory properties. By using these creams, women can reduce their reliance on pain medication, improve their quality of life, and enjoy increased mobility and comfort during their menstrual cycles.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to endometriosis pain that are treated with herbal creams are dysmenorrhea, mastitis, and lactic acid buildup. Herbal creams containing ingredients like arnica, chamomile, and peppermint can help alleviate symptoms such as cramping, inflammation, and discomfort associated with these conditions. These natural remedies can provide relief from painful menstruation, breast engorgement, and skin irritation caused by lactic acid accumulation.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with endometriosis pain is a blend of Willow Bark cream, which contains salicylic acid, and Black Cohosh cream, rich in triterpenes and flavonoids. Combining these with a topical cream containing Turmeric, which has anti-inflammatory properties, and Ginger, known for its pain-relieving abilities, can provide significant relief. Additionally, incorporating a cream with Boswellia, also known for its anti-inflammatory effects, can help soothe and calm the affected areas.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by European review for medical and pharmacological sciences, researchers conducted a systematic review of 11 studies that evaluated the effects of herbal compounds on various aspects of endometriosis treatment. The comprehensive search included databases such as PubMed/MEDLINE, Web of Science Core Collection, and Scopus, and the studies were identified based on keywords such as "Endometriosis" and "Herbal Medicine". The results showed that in addition to safety, drugs available alongside treatments could play an effective role in improving the symptoms associated with endometriosis, including pain, which is a common symptom experienced by women with endometriosis.
